summary: Successfully updated Skills/pulse/SKILL.md with comprehensive documentation for all 4 new Pulse features: Forward Broadcast, Hypothesis Racing, Delegate-Only Mode, and Task Pool (Dynamic Claiming). Added sections with usage examples, updated meta.json structure, and maintained consistent formatting throughout.
decisions: Inserted new feature sections after the 'Learnings System' section to maintain logical document flow
Updated meta.json structure section to include all new fields: delegate_only, first_wins, hypothesis_group, broadcasts, task_pool
Included practical examples for each feature to aid understanding
Referenced the detailed delegate-only-mode.md file rather than duplicating its content
Used consistent markdown formatting and JSON examples matching the existing document style
learnings: Forward Broadcast enables async knowledge sharing between Drops without coordination overhead
Hypothesis Racing provides clean competition mechanism for parallel theory testing
Delegate-Only Mode enforces clear provenance and prevents orchestrator interference
Task Pool enables dynamic work distribution for uniform tasks without pre-planning
notes_for_orchestrator: SKILL.md is now fully updated with all 4 new features. The documentation includes practical examples and maintains backward compatibility. All new features are clearly explained with usage patterns and best practices.
